mission.title: Install and Configure KubeRay
mission.description: KubeRay is an open-source Kubernetes operator that simplifies the deployment and management of Ray applications on Kubernetes. It is ideal for users looking to run distributed machine learning workloads efficiently. Key trade-offs include the need for familiarity with Kubernetes and the potential complexity of managing Ray clusters.

steps.title: Add the KubeRay Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the KubeRay Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install KubeRay using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add kuberay https://ray-project.github.io/kuberay

helm repo update

```

> This adds the official KubeRay Helm repository.
> Updates your local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install KubeRay CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for KubeRay to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ray-project/kuberay/v1.5.1/deploy/crds/raycluster.ray.io_rayclusters.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ray-project/kuberay/v1.5.1/deploy/crds/rayjob.ray.io_rayjobs.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ray-project/kuberay/v1.5.1/deploy/crds/rayservice.ray.io_rayservices.yaml

```

> Installs the RayCluster CRD.
> Installs the RayJob CRD.
> Installs the RayService CRD.

steps.title: Install KubeRay using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs the KubeRay operator in the specified namespace, enabling it to manage Ray resources.

```bash

helm install kuberay kuberay/kuberay --namespace kuberay --create-namespace --version 1.5.1

```

> Installs KubeRay in the 'kuberay' namespace.

steps.title: Verify KubeRay installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the KubeRay operator is running correctly by listing the pods in the KubeRay namespace.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kuberay

```

> You should see the KubeRay operator pod running.

steps.title: Create a sample RayCluster
steps.description:
This step creates a sample RayCluster to verify that KubeRay is functioning as expected.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: ray.io/v1alpha1
kind: RayCluster
metadata:
  name: example-raycluster
  namespace: kuberay
spec:
  head:
    replicas: 1
  worker:
    replicas: 2
EOF

```

> Creates a RayCluster with 1 head and 2 worker nodes.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: ray.io/v1alpha1
kind: RayCluster
metadata:
  name: example-raycluster
  namespace: kuberay
spec:
  head:
    replicas: 1
  worker:
    replicas: 2
EOF

steps.title: Check RayCluster status
steps.description:
This step verifies the status of the created RayCluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get rayclusters -n kuberay

```

> You should see the status of the RayCluster as 'Running'.

uninstall.title: Uninstall KubeRay
uninstall.description:
This step removes KubeRay and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all RayClusters and their data.

```bash

helm uninstall kuberay --namespace kuberay

kubectl delete namespace kuberay

```

> Uninstalls the KubeRay operator.
> Deletes the KubeRay namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade KubeRay
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important data. This step will upgrade KubeRay to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get rayclusters -n kuberay -o yaml > raycluster-backup.yaml

helm upgrade kuberay kuberay/kuberay --namespace kuberay --version 1.5.1

kubectl get pods -n kuberay

```

> Backup existing RayCluster configurations.
> Upgrades KubeRay to the specified version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get rayclusters -n kuberay -o yaml > raycluster-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create RayCluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to insufficient resources in the Kubernetes cluster.

**Fix:**
Ensure your cluster meets the minimum resource requirements (2 vCPU, 4GB RAM per node).

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'KubeRay operator not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The KubeRay operator may not be installed or is not running.

**Fix:**
Run 'kubectl get pods -n kuberay' to check the operator's status and reinstall if necessary.

troubleshooting.title: RayCluster status shows 'Pending'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if there are not enough available nodes to schedule the RayCluster pods.

**Fix:**
Check node availability with 'kubectl get nodes' and ensure they are in 'Ready' state.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl apply failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to invalid YAML syntax in the resource definition.

**Fix:**
Validate your YAML syntax and ensure all required fields are correctly specified.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new RayJob features, improved autoscaling capabilities, and enhanced logging.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the RayCluster status as 'Running' when everything is working correctly.

spec:

    replicas: 1
  worker:
    replicas: 2
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the required tools installed.
